entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hello Goran,
According to the screenshot Actions per Role in Features from section 6. SAP Cloud ALM roles, someone with only role Project Member, under User Management tile, Roles-> Implementation -> Project Management, can do everything except create transports and deploy transports.

For the moment under User Management tile, Roles-> Implementation -> Change & Deployment Management we can select roles:
- Change manager: which allows a user to select action Approve for Production
- Deployment manager: which allows a user to deploy transports into both test and production systems or into only test systems or only into production system

The change manager and deployment manager role needs to be combined with either the project member or the project lead role.

"2nd person can approve it" -> the Project Lead and Project Admin are the only roles that allows to run actions Start Implementation (status In Implemetnation) and Handover to Test (status In Testing)

In Q22024 is expected to get a "New feature status to confirm successful test" according to the roadmap https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002513&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q2%202024

Please check the last webinar for SAP Cloud ALM from June 5, Moritz was speaking about this new role in Deployment, the recording will be available here soon https://events.sap.com/sap-alm-community-call/en/replays

But this is the authorizations granularity delivered from the moment.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative

Hi @Ritesh911 ,

thanks for writing! I've sent an invite your way.

Are you thinking about going into industry consulting?

entry.title: Re: Supply Creation-Based Confirmation (SBC) in SAP S/4HANA-aATP
entry.content: Hi quickly checking, is MATP check possible in aATP or is it in aATP roadmap for future release?

entry.title: How to capture the event of a user pressing Ctrl+A to clear a SAP B1 form's fields
entry.content:
I am trying to capture the event of a user pressing Ctrl+A to clear a SAP Business One form's fields when that form is in focus/active.

I have tried the below but it is not catching the event.

if (pVal.FormTypeEx == "CDD" && pVal.EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoEventTypes.et_KEY_DOWN && pVal.Modifiers == SAPbouiCOM.BoModifiersEnum.mt_CTRL && pVal.CharPressed == 065)
{
    //code
}

How do I accomplish this?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: 42 Berlin Tech Education Summit - Summary
entry.content:
Hi @andre_biener ,

Thanks for sharing! Amongst many newcomers to the SAP ecosystem, there is this perception of potential employers never quite being open to offering "entry level" positions - instead it is a position asking for 4-6 years of experience at entry-level pay. I've seen anecdotes of this, but cannot really tell if it is a justified perception or not.

Was this discussed at all during the session? I'd be curious to get the industry's perspective on this.

entry.title: How to prevent a sales order in C4C to retransfer to S4 when "Send E-mail" is used?
entry.content:
Hello,

Let us say, we have a sales order in C4C that has been transferred to S4 and got confirmed with an S4 ID.

If we use the function "Send E-Mail", the order is set to "In Process" and transferred to S4 again. 

is there a way to prevent this?

thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP WEB IDE Orion Crashing
entry.content:
Hi,

 Can you please extract the webide zip in the following path with  an extractor like 7zip:

C:\SAPWebIDE

entry.title: Re: My experience in digital skills initiative programme
entry.content: Hi @NiteeshReddy - looks like this is a duplicate! https://community.sap.com/t5/beginner-corner-blog-posts/my-experience-in-digital-skills-initiative/ba-p/13716150

entry.title: Re: Virtual element not refreshed on Save RAP SAP
entry.content:
Hi Wolfgang,

Thanks for your reply.

I just need to refresh the page. Do you have any blog or steps how to reload/refresh the page on save for Non-Draft.

Regards,

Santosh F.

entry.title: Notification of leaver action, before workflow approval takes place
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to find a solution to notify our IT team when a leaver action has been triggered for any employee in employee central; we do already have a 2 step workflow approval set up (which IT are not a part of and don’t need to be) - but we want to notify them BEFORE the workflow is approved as it can sometime be delayed causing potential security risks, and we may need to remove IT access from the leaver before their leaving date. I also want to be able to use a custom notification with specific information on it to send to IT (i.e. not use the standard notification template).

  • I don’t want to add them as an additional approver as this could slow the process even further and there is no approval required by them - is just for visibility that a leaver action has been triggered.
  • I have tried adding them as a contributor to the workflow, but that will only allow the standard notification template to be used.
  • I have tried adding multiple workflows to the leaver action - but that is not possible (as per SAP notes https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3286215#:~:text=Resolution,one%20workflow%20triggering%20per%20transaction)
  • Reporting is too late - we need to know as the action is being triggered not after the fact.

Does anyone else have a similar requirement or know of anything else I can try?

Many thanks in advance!

Liz

entry.title: Re: How to display additional columns in the report list of a Sales Fiori app?
entry.content: How to reset to default shows additonal fields?

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI LAUNCH PAD URL
entry.content: Please run the t-code: "/n/ui2/flp" and it will open the launchpad in your default browser.

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

Great Blog!!

I created a similar hierarchy in datasphere and used it in one of the fact views. It has multiple hierarchies. It works perfectly fine

However, I keep only 1 hierarchy in Hier with Dir and create a variable on top of the object from the fact view which has an association to the Dimension View which in turn has an Hier with Dir Association to the Hier With Dir View. Now the variable does not show the Hierarchy but just the Flat presentation of the values.

Do you know if it is possible to show the hierarchy even in the variable using this method of Hierarchy creation? If not, which method of hierarchy creation is most suitable if we want the hierarchy to be visible even in variables.

Your inputs are much appreciated.

Thank You.

entry.title: Eclipse: How to see / change function module documentation?
entry.content: In SAPGUI there exists "Function Module Documentation". Is there a way in Eclipse to accessed and maintain it?

entry.title: SAP Developer News, June 6th, 2024
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Code Connect 2024

  • https://code-connect.dev/

DYK #23  Bookmark for BAS dev spaces

ABAP Tools - Configure Tool Bridge

  • https://software-heroes.com/en/blog/abap-tools-work-with-ecplise-configure-tool-bridge
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-may-2024-release-adds-closer-integration-with-abap/ba-p/13720761

Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite, AEM

  • Learning journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-event-driven-integration-with-sap-integration-suite-advanced-event-mesh
  • Event-driven integrations CodeJam: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/
  • Request a CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415

ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) Cheat Sheet

  • https://d.dam.sap.com/a/UgpGkLH/Cheat_Sheet.pdf?rc=10&inline=true&doi=SAP1083288
  • https://www.brandeis.de/en/blog/cheat-sheet-cds-abap

June Developer Challenge: Multi-model in SAP HANA Cloud, incl. the Vector Engine

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-sap-hana-multi-model-using-python-in-sap-business/ba-p/13722560

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:07 Code Connect 2024

0:46 DYK #23  Bookmark for BAS dev spaces

1:56 ABAP Tools - Configure Tool Bridge + SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release

3:29 Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite, AEM

4:37 ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) Cheat Sheet

5:35 June Developer Challenge: Multi-model in SAP HANA Cloud, incl. the Vector Engine

TRANSCRIPT

[DJ and Nico] Hi everyone and welcome from Code Connect 2024. It's been a fantastic week so far. We had a Cap Code Jam on Monday, we had ReCap on Tuesday, and today, what is today? It's UI5Con. It's UI5Con today. Two awesome keynote sessions already, one from Daniel yesterday and one from Peter and the gang this morning, right? And what else have we got? And we're looking forward to a lot more sessions, a lot We're networking and talking to friends and colleagues, and we hope to see you soon, next year, hopefully at Code Connect 25. Yeah, Code Connect 25, be there.

[Ajay] Did you know SAP Business Application Studio allows users to create development space tailored for specific development scenarios? When you create a dev space, it is associated with your user account, and you can access it through the BAS dashboard. If you have a bookmark to your specific DevSpace, you can indeed access it more quickly bypassing the need to navigate to the initial BAS dashboard. If your DevSpace is not running, clicking the bookmark will take you to a page where you can start the DevSpace. Starting a DevSpace can take a moment because the underlying resources need to be provisioned and initialized. If your dev space is already run in, clicking the bookmark will take you directly to the dev space environment, where you can continue with your work without any delay. Bookmarking your dev space is a convenient way to save time and reduce the number of clicks needed to access your development environment.

[Mamikee] Hello, everyone. I'm coming to you live from ABAP Conference in Germany on this beautiful Thursday, June 6th. We have a new blog post to check out. In the latest blog post, our community member speaks about the powerful tool bridge, the future in the ABAP development tools version 3.42. Learn how this integration with Business Application Studio or VS Code can revolutionize your ABAP development workflow. This blog post highlights what the tool bridge is and the significance in ABAP development, step-by-step instructions to set up the tool bridge with BAS, tips on using the tool bridge to enhance your development process, and many more. Don't miss out on this opportunity to improve your ABAP development process. Check out the link in the description below. Also, don't forget to check out this blog post by Ian Wigley where he discovers the latest enhancement in the SAP PURE tools May 2024 release, which brings closer integration with ABAP development tools with the 24.05 client release ADT and the latest SAP PURE tools. You can now enjoy a more seamless experience between backend and frontend development. In this blog post, Ian Quigley explains how the integration simplifies the development process. Read Ian Quigley's full blog post in the link below.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Are you interested in learning about Event Driven Architectures and SAP Integration Suite Advanced Vendors? Well, you're in luck, because this week we recently released a new learning journey focusing on that. learning journey is called Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Management. You will go through the basics on what Event-Driven Architectures is and also you will check out all the different components that are part of SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Management. For example, Issue Control, the Event Portal, and all the different event sources that there are for Event-Driven Architectures in the SAP ecosystem. Be sure to check it out. But what about some hands-on experience for the product? Well, there's also the Event-Reviewing Interpretions with SAP Interpretation Suite CodeGen that you can also request if you want to host an event and that we can get some hands-on experience of the tool. I'm including all the links in the description below.

[Sheena] Hi, everyone. As developers, having a comprehensive overview of syntax and its descriptions in a single document is extremely valuable to us. Andrea Schlotthauer has put together all this information about ABAP Core Data Services in the form of a cheat sheet. This ABAP CDS cheat sheet consists of the overview of all available CDS objects and entities delivered as part of ABAP platform 2023. It also lists down the annotations, associations, data types and type conversions, session variables, and also the operands and expressions that are available in the select list of a Vue entity. In addition, it includes links to numerous knowledge resources for ABAP CDS. So enjoy faster coding by referring to the ABAP CDS cheat sheet linked in the description below.

[Witalij] Hello, it is June already, and it means it's time to recap last month's developer challenge and turn out the new one. During a few weeks of May, you could participate in the developer challenge that was prepared by my dear friend Nora, and you could learn how to use SAP Business AI services, like premium version of document information extraction or data attribute recommendation. A few dozen of you, including actually myself, took part in that with almost 25% going through all five weeks' challenges. Now it's time to announce the new one. So hello, all fellow data geeks, and I'm really happy to announce that this month the challenge will be around SAP HANA multimodal capabilities, including obviously the new Vector engine. The very first week has been published already. And it is all about the setup to get you familiar with SAP Business Application Studio and how it works with Python, Jupyter, and how it can be and should be configured once you are working on a project where you are mining data from SAP HANA Cloud. So I saw the first submission already. It made me really happy and I'm looking for more submissions from all of you.

entry.title: RAP - Unable to navigate to Object Page in FLP but works fine with VS Code
entry.content:
I am pre-filling header and child entities with static factory action and abstract entity. I am filling Reported and Failed Structure if valid date is not entered. If valid data is entered, I am creating header and Child entities and filling Mapped Structure.  While testing, I  gave invalid data and Failed structure is filled fine and errors are shown fine. Now, When I correct the data, the header and child entity is created with Mapped Structure filled fine BUT the navigation to the object is not happening. The dialog screen is getting closed, draft table entry(for both Header and Child) is getting created but no navigation to the object page. 

This issue is only when I am running it in Fiori launchpad. When running locally with VS code, no issue. I see entities created fine and navigation is perfect.

Did anyone come across the issue of navigation in VS code vs FLP? 

#RAP #SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: DDMRP prioritized share
entry.content:
Hi,

The scenario described is very simplistic assuming:

  • DDMRP replenishment taking place at the same time for all plants,
  • The material is buffered in all plants,
  • The supplier is the same for all plants (and there is no supplying alternative for any of the plants).

If you consider that the replenishment run can be plant specific, that the material is not buffered in all plants, and that the (preferred) supplier can differ by plant (which all can be modelled in S/4), the scenario becomes quite complex to handle in business terms.

There is no such prioritized share consideration in SAP S/4HANA Demand-Driven Replenishment taking supplier constraints into consideration.

entry.title: Re: Report for Email address
entry.content: Thank you Donnie, your solution was was useful.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: How to see / change function module document...
entry.content: Press F2, click on link.

entry.title: Re: Lookup of function RODPS_REPL_CONTEXT_GET_LIST metada...
entry.content:
Anyone who is struggling with the 'Lookup of function xxxx metadata failed for destination xxx' error:

I was able to resolve this one by

1. downloading the latest version of the SAP Connector for Microsoft .NET

2. installing it

3. updating my .NET project by adding a reference to sapnco.dll and sapnco_utils.dll which could be found in C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\assembly\GAC_64 (each with their own subfolder)

entry.title: Re: Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 3-System Landscape: From Basics to Comp
entry.content:
Hey,

 

yes, it works. Thanks!

entry.title: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Content Navigation

Part 1: A simple UI5 app that runs in the SAP Launchpad Service 

Part 2: Multiple Apps with a Shared Reuse Library  

Part 3: Splitting bigger projects 

Part 4: Splitting the Destination Service instance (Current page) 

Splitting the Destination Service instance 

Current State

In the last posts you have learned how to split multiple apps into multiple app-hosts and xsuaa instances which are deployed by multiple MTAs. This setup allows app developers to work independently and even define their own authorization models. 

However, they still share the same instance of the destination service, and all destinations are created there.  

This instance still acts as a common context for all apps. For example, the URLs to access these apps contain the instance GUID, which is the same for all (this has been explained in part 2 of this blog). 

Reasons for the split 

In most cases, this setup works just fine. But there's a potential for conflicts, and that's when you need to split into multiple destination service instances. 
One such conflict can arise from multiple instances of the same business services: 

Let us assume there is a service with an OData endpoint that allows us to store documents. 
Every instance of this service has its own isolated area where the documents are stored and only provides access to them. These documents are protected by the credentials in the service key of the instance.  

The clientId and clientsecret are specific for this instance and are used by the service to separate the stored data. 

Here is an excerpt of how the service key could look like: 

{ 
    "endpoints": { 
        "api": "https://<URL>/api" 
    }, 
    "grant_type": "user_token", 
    "sap.cloud.service": "btp.samples.consumed.service", 
    "uaa": { 
        "clientid": "<...>", 
        "clientsecret": "<...>", 
        "url": "<...>", 
        "serviceInstanceId": "<...>" 
    } 
} 

This key will then be converted into a destination on the destination service instance to make it available to the managed app router. 

To connect to the OData endpoint of the service, the app adds a routing configuration to its xs-app.json: 

 { 
      "source": "^/api/documents/info$", 
      "target": "info", 
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa", 
      "service": "btp.samples.consumed.service", 
      "endpoint": "api" 
}, 

As you can see, the routing refers to the service key by specifying the service name that appears in the service credentials as sap.cloud.service and then chooses a service endpoint. 
The contained credentials are then used to get an access token and call the service. 

Now let us further assume that app1 and app2 both have their own instance of the document service.  
You must ensure that both apps access their dedicated content by using the credentials of their instance, i.e., they must access the services within their context. 
Without splitting the destination service instance, both destinations for the document service would be created on the shared instance. 

Unfortunately, this is now causing conflicts because both destinations have the same sap.cloud.service specified and the resolution of the service keys to use is ambiguous. I.e., the destination service instance defines only one context under which the service can be accessed. 

This is the moment where you must split the destination service instance to get multiple contexts to access the service (one for app1 and one for app2). 

How to split 

After the split, you no longer have a shared destination service instance. Instead, each context gets its own. The destinations related to the apps are moved to these instances and the destinations for the shared library are now duplicated and appear on both instances. Additionally, there are destinations for the consumed service instances.  

Let us see how the MTAs have changed [Shared, App1, App2]. 

Here the resource for the destination instance has been removed from the library MTA and switched to managed instances in the app MTAs. Additionally, the app MTAs now refer to the library app-host and xsuaa as existing service.  
Each of them contains the destinations of their app-host and xsuaa instances as well as for the shared app-host and xsuaa. 
Also, there are now the resources for the consumed service and the destination definition. 

In our sample, the consumed service offers a very simple endpoint that just returns information about the access token used. 
The apps have an additional datasource and model defined in their manifest.json to read this, and the clientId is now bound to the custom control in the master.view.xml to be shown on the UI. 

Access at runtime 

Because you’ve split the destination instance, the runtime access URLs change. Now, each app URL includes the GUID of its destination instance. 
This way the resolution of sap.cloud.service names is uniquely resolved via the correct destination to use the correct credentials. 

When running the apps they display the used clientid. You can verify that both apps show a different one, which reflects the instance GUID of the consumed service. 

Well done, now we have achieved a complete separation of the two apps.

entry.title: Re: Automatically update SSL Certificates before they expire in SAP CPI
entry.content: Hi, I am trying to implement this iFlow, but on the second Request it retrieves me a 401 error - Unauthorized. The user credentials are the same as in the first request, maybe the token is not passed correctly to the request's headers (value=Fetch), but it is well retrieved from the first call:

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content: Once the message/event arrives on AEM as the service for federating enterprise events, they could be shared easily with Azure and/or Kafka. Embedded as part of the AEM service is a Kakfa bridge component to allow events to easily be shared with Kafka.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: How to see / change function module document...
entry.content: I didn't mean the parameter definition but the function module definition long text where you can describe the function module and it's parameters as longtext

entry.title: Not getting create option in Data Provisioning in SLT for CDS View.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Issue: Source is S/4 PERSON_NAME with version DIGITS and SLT DMIS is DIGITS SP09 and Target is PERSON_NAME 2.0.

I want to create CDS view , but I am getting OLD UI in Data Provisioning option. So the query is why we are not getting new UI of Data Provisioning in SLT so that we can create CDS View.

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not getting create option in Data Provisioning in SLT...
entry.content:
Hello,

To switch to the new Data Provisioning UI you have to run the report "IUUC_REPL_SWITCH_REC_MCHNSM", for this report to work you have to make sure there are no objects added to the configuration so first remove the objects from the configuration then run the report in SE38

https://me.sap.com/notes/3169933

Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How can I configure SSO with Kerberos in the old way...
entry.content: But this is to use SSO 2.0 and 3.0 where I have to use the Secure Login Client/Server.

entry.title: Re: How to share IQ multiplex DBFiles opened with (O_DIRE...
entry.content: I'm not clear on what your question is then. If you are asking if raw devices are supported when SAP IQ is configured as a multiplex system, then the documentation clearly states that yes, raw devices are supported. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_IQ/a8986af484f21015b55bed80129dc962/a242aca184f2101585f4d110845df83a.html?q=raw%20device

entry.title: Re: What is the Better way for developing Reports in SAP...
entry.content:
you should go with embedded analytics

gui/alv/dynpro should be the least favored.

entry.title: Re: BO 4.3 Cloud - No such file or directory
entry.content: Have you filled in the “Destination_AllowList.txt”-file correctly?

entry.title: Re: Introducing Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @SachinBG - sure, you are able to do so by adding a customer specific IDP (abc.com) under the customer entity.
Once done, It will then be possible to add IDP users (e.g. user@abc.com) under that customer entity.
Hope this helps
Best Rgs
Guy

entry.title: Cloud ALM external log open telemetry collector forwarding to Splunk
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to find out if a specific scenario is possible in SAP Cloud ALM.

We have an API in SAP BTP Integration studio and wish to call the SAP ALM logging endpoint to log a message  ( I believe the endpoint being https://{tenant}.eu10.alm.cloud.sap/api/calm-logs/v1/logs  ) I want this to then hit the open telemetry collector queue in SAP ALM and be forwarded to a logging provider ( again configured in ALM ) - to send this log message to e.g splunk 

The log once accepted by Splunk would then be deleted in ALM 

Would any be able to advise if this is possible with ALM ?

the advantage of this is if the logging solution is changed then this would be reconfigured in ALM and not all of the API / CPI integrations 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
The URL of your HANA db SQL endpoint

3d11-492b-bdcd-3189b1067789.hana.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443

is a bit too short and is missing 4 alphanumeric characters at the beginning.

Can you double-check if it matches the SQL Endpoint of your HANA db?

You can see me getting the same error if I remove a few characters from the beginning of my HANA db endpoint.

entry.title: Re: How can I configure SSO with Kerberos in the old way...
entry.content:
The SNC client is needed to read which user is logged on the computer and pass that information to the SAP GUI login procedure.

If you go to the SAP GUI connection definition, change to the second tab (Network), you will see that the "Single Sign On" option is only available if you activate the "Secure Network Communication".

entry.title: Re: Consuming data from SAP Datasphere into Power BI via ODBC connector
entry.content: what about power bi service or power bi cloud? were you able to publish a PBI report in the cloud and to schedule the refresh from datasphere?

Kajal Sahu

entry.title: Re: how to copy a flavor from one t-code to another t-code?
entry.content: However, copying a flavor from MIGO to MM01 doesn't make sense. While technically speaking it's possible, the two transactions have completely different module pools and screens, so the new flavor will not be usable.

entry.title: Re: sub contracting
entry.content: Mr. Ninad, Could you please explain it in detail??

entry.title: V2 oData CAP services, fetching data from external HDI are very unstable
entry.content:
I am building CAP app that fetches the data from remote HDI container. I need oData V2 services to use them in MDK app. CAP provides only oData V4 which I tried to convert to V2 with different adapters (like @cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter, @sap/cds-odata-v2-adapter-proxy, @sap/cds-odata-v2-adapter-proxy )  but with all of them I am facing the same issue. The CAP app is deployed in Cloud Foundry.

The problem is: the call to the service many times works and many doesn't, it seams like very unstable, it gives me error like 'Service not available: objects' or '500 Internal Server Error' or 'Not Found". There is no pattern when it works and when it doesn't, but very often it doesn't. I am not excluding the fact that maybe between the data source and the CAP app is the problem, but if I try to open the data in the database explorer it always works fine. Any help or thoughts are very appreciated.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Thanks Vitaliy.

It was issue with URL i did not notice.This worked for me.

entry.title: Re: V2 oData CAP services, fetching data from external HD...
entry.content: Why not just use OData V4?  MDK works with V4 as well as V2 in offline and online mode.  If there is something specific with V4 that isn't working please let us know.

entry.title: Re: SAP IS-U FICA:Setting Conditional Locks at Line item Level
entry.content: Hi I am curious as how to make a tcode trigger from the code ? would you have an example ?

entry.title: Re: V2 oData CAP services, fetching data from external HD...
entry.content: Thank you very much Bill for your fast answer. The reason I want OData V2 is because the MDK app is already created before and it used to work with V2 services. Do you know, if I change the OData to V4 now, what changes are required in the MKD app, or is it feasible to keep the old MKD app at all?

entry.title: Syntax Error in GRC Dev standard code
entry.content:
Hi!!!

In my DEV GRC Server  when i assign a Firefigther, I get the following error:

500 SAP Internal Server Error
ERROR: Syntax error in program CL_GRAC_POWL_SPM_FFOBJECT=====CP . (termination: RABAX_STATE)

entry.title: Re: STO Retorno - Transferência na localização Brasil
entry.content:
Olá Patricia @Eidelwein , 

Estou com o mesmo problema reportado pelo Edmilson Zaghi:


" Ao efetuar um processo de Retorno o SAP utiliza o PMM atual do produto e temos casos onde o PMM desse produto sofre alterações, e ao realizar o Retorno os valores entre a saída e o retorno ficam divergentes.

Existe alguma solução para o processo de Retorno espelhar os valores (Fiscal e Contábil) da saída e não utilizar o PMM atual ? " 

 

Atualmente já existe alguma solução para esse situação? 

Desde já agradeço pela atenção.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: Getting below error now.

entry.title: Re: Process Automation Desktop Agent for MAC
entry.content: Yes, it is. Confirmed.

entry.title: Re: Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution rea...
entry.content:
Hi akhthaaralibadhusha,

Are you trying to run automation in unattended mode after deployment?. If yes please check if you have set the desktop agent "Mode" to unattended.

As per your screenshots - there are no unattended agents found for the job to be distributed. And your previous successful run was in "attended" mode. 

 

Alternatively, check if you have unattended bot entitlement assigned for your subaccount where you are executing the automation.

 

Regards,

Vishal Rathi

entry.title: Grant SYSTEM PRIVILEGES like EXPORT or IMPORT into a (HDI) HANA DEPLOYMENT CONTAINER
entry.content:
In order to get the system privileges someone has to give them to your HDI container. This are the steps you have to follow to get them into the container. In this a sample the IMPORT/EXPORT privileges could be given.

  1.  [optional] A user with DBA-ADMIN role creates a usergroup  [HELP] such as "HDI_CONTAINER_RIGHTS"
    Either use the HANA Cloud Central to create a USERGROUPor use a SQL command:

  2. -- DROP USERGROUP "HDI_CONTAINER_RIGHTS";

    
CREATE USERGROUP "HDI_CONTAINER_RIGHTS";
  3. Create a USER "HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTOR":
    or use a SQL command:
    --DROP USER HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTER;

    
CREATE USER HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTER



    PASSWORD Abcd1234 NO FORCE_FIRST_PASSWORD_CHANGE 



    SET USERGROUP "HDI_CONTAINER_RIGHTS";
  4. Define a ROLE "HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI" that has the privileges needed for the container:

    or use and SQL command:
    CREATE ROLE HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI;

    GRANT IMPORT TO HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI;

    GRANT EXPORT TO HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI;
  5. Now this role has to be given to the HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTOR with GRANT to others.
    He will give the HDI container the role so that the container can import and export
    or with the SQL commend:
    GRANT HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI TO HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTER WITH ADMIN OPTION;

    Now we have done the HANA Cloud side of things.
    There is a user "HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTER" that can grant a role "HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI" with  IMPORT and EXPORT privileges.
  6. Now we have to get this role into our HDI container. We have to switch to the BTP Cockpit:
    Inside your development space -here dev - a CUPS (Custom User Provided Service) needs to be defined.
    {
       "user": "HDI_IMPORT_EXPORT_GRANTER",
       "password": "Abcd1234",
       "tags": [
          "hana"
       ]
    }

     

    Now there is a CUPS service in your space that could grant your HDI Containers the role to import and export. Of course you need all this in a similar style in other landscapes you are using. Explain this to your SAP HANA Cloud ADMIN. Certainly there are only very few people in production allowed to see the passwords. The rights of the role had been selected by intention very limited.
  7. Now we go into BusinessApplicationStudio to get the wanted role into the HDI Container.
    1. Invoke the CUPS service into your project:

       

      That will appear as a "cross-container-service-n". (In my case I had another one as well)
      This container get a name: here "cross-container-service-3" which you will also find back in the mta.yaml file:

       

    2. Now that we have a service that could GRANT us the role we are looking for we need a hdbgrants file:

      or here the text version:

      {
         "ServiceName_3":
            {
                "object_owner":{
                     "global_roles": [
                         {
                           "roles": [
                              "HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI"
                           ]
                         }
                      ]
                },
                "application_user": {
                     "global_roles": [
                         {
                           "roles": [
                              "HANAIMPORTEXPORT4HDI"
                      ]
                  }
               ]
            }
         }
      }
      (Formatting is maybe wrong but content should be ok
    3.  Next step is deployment
      The result shows that we successful granted the hdbgrants files:

       

  8. Now the import /export can be tested: Open the DBX application as RT user:

    and the prices are now imported and exported through my S3 bucket ( as BTP object store service)

  9. There is a PSE/Certificate setting that needed to be defined as well. Please check with below BLOG to establish this HANA Cloud setting.
    BLOG on copy HDI containers explains how to get a certificate and create a PSE

Related Blogs:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/create-virtual-table-in-hdi/ba-p/13577953

Summary:

To Import/Export table data in HDI you need:

  • An object store ( easiest and best within BTP Object Store service )
  • A granter user with a role in HANA Cloud that has IMPORT and EXPORT privileges.
  • A CUPS definition in your BTP SPACE
  • A database connection in your project that adds the CUPS service to the project
  • A hdbgrantsfile that takes the role for the application user [and HDI owner]

Now a SQL command by the application user can import or export tables.

entry.title: Re: Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution rea...
entry.content: What is the difference between attended and unattended bot ? Can you please explain or point out to a blog where it is explained ?

entry.title: Re: Delay in courses becoming searchable through the new learning homepage
entry.content: I am having the same issue. Does anyone know why this is happing or have a suggestion?

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content:
Hi @anantapalli , thank you for sharing this blog.

I have 2 questions regarding custom tasks on onboarding. 

1- We configured custom tasks on onboarding for several roles such as manager, onboardee, custom managers. But all those roles can see each others' custom tasks and I am using seperate objects for each and seperate permission roles. Do you have any idea what could be the problem?

2- We are having "There was an application error - ???COMMON_Ok???" error for some custom tasks but we can complete others. Even though the objects are configured the same, one custom task could be done but the other couldn't. I would really appreciate if you can suggest any solution.

Thank you.

entry.title: I am unable to print multi level BOM output after material is configured in advance VC in public clo
entry.content:
Hi,

we are implementing SAPS4 HANA public cloud edition with Variant Configurator , however as there is no CDS view to print Multi level bom, please suggest the solution

entry.title: Re: Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution rea...
entry.content:
Hi, In simple terms:

--Attended Execution/Bot > requires human intervention to complete automation process. Usually deployed on worker’s desktop/laptop. Can think of it as a digital assistant to the user.

--Unattended Execution/bot> runs without human intervention. Usually deployed remotely on say a server. Can think of it as a individual digital worker for organization.

Reference links

  1. how to change agent mode here.
  2. SAP Note

 

Hope this helps..

entry.title: Re: BO 4.3 Cloud - No such file or directory
entry.content: Hello Nachlaktiv! Thank you for your help. The Report runs correctly most of the time, but every other time there is an error, it is always the same report and the same destination.

entry.title: Re: SDI Configuration: Connect Data Provision Agent(SDI) to Cloud Foundry environment and enable the
entry.content:
***** The GUI above has been deprecated by SAP.*****

Only interface for configuration is 

a - Windows) agentcli.bat --configAgent

b - UNIX/LINUX) agentcli.sh --configAgent

entry.title: Re: Is the mixed costing functionality an option or avail...
entry.content:
Hi Team,

The option of Mixed Costing is not available in the current version of S/4HANA Public Cloud. Although, you can raise a feature request for this business requirement. Kindly check the below KBA:

2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product

Kind Regards,
Helena

entry.title: Re: OData service unable to pass MD parameter
entry.content: https://my400658-api.scmibp.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/IBP/EXTRACT_ODATA_SRV/LLS4P700?$select=PRDID,PRDDESCR

entry.title: Re: OData service unable to pass MD parameter
entry.content: In Power BI the query doesn't work. Adding &$filter=MASTER_DATA_TYPE eq '[MASTERDATA]' or &$filter=MASTER_DATA_TYPE eq '[MASTERDATA]'&saml2=disabled didn't help.

entry.title: Re: Introduction of SAC Multi-actions Public API
entry.content: Hi, any clue regarding workarounds for the depricated SystemUser issue? In postman I get the following response when requesting a token for SAC:

entry.title: How do I make the effective price not the lowest quantity on tiered pricing?
entry.content:
Hello,

We receive tiered pricing for an item.  for example, the tiers are 

qty 3000  - $900.00 /1,000 units

qty 5000 - $ 700.00/1,000 units

qty 10,000 - $400.00/1,000 units

qty 25000 - $200.00/1,000 units

We usually buy the product at the 10,000 increment, which would be $400.00 per 1,000 units.  I would like that denomination to show up in ME12 for net price and effective price, as that is what is used for costing. 

I would like to also put the entire tiered pricing into the conditions PB00, however when  I do that, it changes the net & effective price to the lowest cost of the tier. 

 

How do I avoid this?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @Maheshwar_Singh,

This is very exciting! However, when we expand Connect to Live Data and we do not see the SAP Cloud Application option. How can we troubleshoot this issue?

Hi @Manuel_Namyslo ,

Thanks for the blog.

Is it possible to define paging for the fetch the data?

Regards

Mauricio

entry.title: Re: RAP - Unable to navigate to Object Page in FLP but wo...
entry.content:
Hi Jun,

No Error message in the console.  Interestingly, if the user enters invalid date , the errors are shown because Reported and Failed Structures are populated. Right after that if user corrects the invalid entries, the navigation is not working. The dialog is getting closed and the user stays in the main page.

 If valid entries are entered to begin with, the navigation is fine.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Also, it appears the link for Availability of Live Connection from SAP SuccessFactors to SAP Analytics Cloud is broken on this page. When will this be corrected?

entry.title: Harmonized Document Management(HDM) for Purchase Requisition in S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello SDN,

I have a requirement for copying attachments from Purchase Requisition to(Doc Type ZSPA and Status- Approved):

1. Request for Quotation (Doc Type ZAN)

2. Purchase Order(Doc Type APO)

Learnt that this can be achieved through HDM. I navigated to the path:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide > ABAP Platform > Application Server > Basis Services > Harmonized Document Management > Activate HDM for Attachment Object Type

I found the below object types:

PURCHASECONTRACT

PURCHASECONTRACTITEM

PURCHASINGQUALITYINFORECORD

Could not find any object type for Purchase Requisition.

Can someone guide me on configuring HDM for Purchase Requisition in S4 HANA?

Regards,

Raj

entry.title: Re: Process order showing double component in materials tab.
entry.content: Thanks for coming back and sharing the solution.

entry.title: Re: MRP not happening through MD01 but executed through MD02

entry.content: You can use concat as described here: Performance Modeling | cap≽ire (cloud.sap)

entry.title: Packaging materials in ewm
entry.content:
Hello ,

I’n our business scenario , we always keep packaging materials in stock for goods issue I.e outbound process to customer . Do we need to maintain packaging materials for inbound process too ? I am not clear with the significance. How the packaging materials are procured in standard ?

entry.title: Schedule Lines not getting deleted when passing Delete Indicator to BAPI : BAPI_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN
entry.content:
Issue:

  • Whenever we are trying deletion of a schedule line , the custom program is passing on the input to BAPI : BAPI_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN with the Scheduling Agreement Number , Item Number and Schedule line and Delete Indicator as ‘X’ to the BAPI.

  •  

    However when this input is sent to BAPI , it is only changing the Quantity to ‘0.00’ for that schedule line and marking the line as fixed and not deleting it.
  • We have observed this is only happening for those Scheduling agreements where releases were generated before by ME84.

Does anyone knows why the lines are not getting deleted and how this can be resolved?

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community in Melbourne - Wednesday 5th June 2024

Weather was nice, chilly and a few sprinkles of rain but great to have a few people running with me this year in Melbourne. 400% improvement on last year 😁

Carol, Sai, Rohan and myself at the Tan

thanks for joining

@Saikrishna_Sriram 

@RohanShukla 

Carol Wallis!

entry.title: Re: First SAP Tech Night Melbourne on 13th June 2024
entry.content:
Hey @custodio the link is above. There is no EventBrite (as they are not charging for events) so just indicate whether you are coming by saying Yes - to the event. 

Here is the link to the event. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-inside-track/first-sap-melbourne-tech-night-june-2024/ev-p/13677153

thanks

Phil

entry.title: Treasury management general ledger accounts in chart of accounts YCOA
entry.content:
Can someone explain the difference between the use of two accounts in chart of accounts YCOA in SAP Treasury:

Derivative Assets
Derivative Value Balance

I have looked at the derivation rules that SAP has supplied and don't see a characteristics to differentiate between the two.

Both accounts are used with derivation rules that specify the product type and the company relationship in company code.

I know this is a very basic question but it has me stumped.   

How does one know from the product type whether the general ledger account for initial posting should be either Derivative assets or Derivative Value Balance. 

I have reviewed SAP Press's SAP Treasury and Risk Management 2nd edition and am unable to find the answer there.

 

GL account   
12041000Deriv Val Bal Uaff
12041200Deriv Val Bal Consld
12041100Deriv Val Bal NCsld
12042000Deriv Assets Unaff
12042000Deriv Assets Unaff
12042000Deriv Assets Unaff

entry.title: Is there any way to track or audit changes on who had deleted the forms
Re: Want to delete DRAFT entries in bulk mode
entry.content:
Dude, no no no no and NO. Forbidden by contract to do this in the SAP database, you can only update simple custom tables, doing this you lose the warranty, in addition to making the system present strange errors because it will not find the data.

I'll attach the notes that you can update here in the reply. But to close multiple sketches, you can go to the sketch report and mark multiple and close (new version of SAP FP 2405) 

https://file.io/Mj1BJ9BMmSFA

Rgs

Fabio.

SAP 9.3 PL14 / SQL SERVER 2014

entry.title: Evaluate promotions without modifying session cart
entry.content:
Is there any way to simulate product-level promotion calculation without modifying the session cart?

I'm using a promotion where adding two specific products to the cart grants different percentage discount on every products. If any of these products also have individual promotions, those discounts are applied as well.

I want to get the final price of the products with the promotions applied, but without modifying the actual shopping cart. I tried using a Groovy to create a temporary cart using cartFactory. I then added the products using abstractOrderService.addNewEntry and calculated the cart with calculationService.calculate. However, the promotions are not being evaluated.

Is there a way to achieve this functionality?
